key_insight: Charged atomic planes in SmNiO3 buffer layers can deterministically polarize ultrathin BaTiO3 films and eliminate the usual critical thickness for ferroelectricity.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical interface-engineering route to stabilize switchable polarization in single-unit-cell ferroelectric devices for nanoscale memories and oxide electronics.
method: Use (001)-oriented SmNiO3 as a polarizing template for BaTiO3 growth.
Select [SmO]+ or [NiO2]- terminations to set upward or downward polarization.
Support the interface design with density-functional-theory calculations and defect analysis.
evidence_signal: Experiments show net polarization appearing from the first BaTiO3 unit cell, consistent with DFT-guided interpretation of termination-dependent interfacial polarization.
watch_item: Oxygen vacancies can weaken or counteract the polarizing effect, so defect control is critical for reliable device implementation.
paper_title: Polarizing ultrathin ferroelectric BaTiO3 films through interfacial layer polarization
